subject: Chemistry
title: Ultramarine: Ground Gemstones
description: A fast breakdown of Ultramarine: Ground Gemstones for builders.

hook.text: Ultramarine blue was made by grinding lapis lazuli gemstones. It cost more than gold in medieval Europe.

buildup.text: Lapis lazuli contains lazurite, a mineral with sulfur trapped inside a silicate cage. The sulfur creates the vivid blue.

discovery.text: The pigment was reserved for the most important figures. The Virgin Mary's robe was always painted in ultramarine.

twist.text: In 1826, a French chemist synthesized ultramarine artificially. The price dropped by 99%, democratizing blue paint.

climax.text: Yves Klein created his own ultramarine formula in 1960—International Klein Blue—and made it his artistic trademark.

punchline.text: Sulfur in a silicate cage. A gemstone's chemistry painted the Renaissance.

quiz.question: What element gives lapis lazuli its blue color?
options: Sulfur atoms trapped inside a silicate mineral cage
Copper ions dissolved in the crystal
